key_matchups: James on the ball against Uzbekistan's organised shape
phases.summary: The opening quarter was cagey and measured, with Colombia comfortable circulating possession across their back four while staying conservative enough to avoid transition danger. Uzbekistan were compact and disciplined without the ball, then more direct when they had it, looking early for long or medium passes into their creative attackers. Set pieces provided the main early chances, but neither side settled into a rhythm.

formations_blocks: Colombia controlling tempo with sustained possession
adjustments: Colombia looking for runners between Uzbekistan's three centre-halves
Arias dropping into between-the-lines pockets and combining with James and Puerta
Colombia attempting more down-the-right overlap and near-post crossing
key_matchups: Arias vs Uzbekistan's defensive block between the lines
phases.summary: Colombia gradually took control of possession and the tempo, but in a slow, low-threat rhythm that repeatedly ran into Uzbekistan’s compact 5-4-1. Their best moments came when Arias found space between the lines or when they advanced down the right, yet Uzbekistan remained organized and difficult to break down, with Lucumi later forced into an important recovery against Shomurodov. The breakthrough eventually arrived via Munoz’s goal in the 41st minute after a phase still largely defined by sterile Colombia circulation and disciplined Uzbek defending.

adjustments: Uzbekistan started to expand their shape as the game wore on
Uzbekistan showed more initiative on the ball after initially sitting back
phases.summary: Uzbekistan spent much of the first half in a compact 5-4-1, prioritising defensive organisation and limiting space, while Colombia dominated territory and possession without being especially incisive. By half-time the Uzbeks were being urged to take more risks in possession, having offered little threat beyond rare breaks and a few failed attempts to find channel runners.

adjustments: Uzbekistan substituted Khamdamov and Sayfiev on for Nasrullaev and Urunov at half-time
Uzbekistan switched Fayzullaev from the right flank to the left to exploit space in behind Munoz
Colombia continued their conservative keep-ball approach before dropping into a low block
Both teams looked to counter after turnovers in transition
phases.summary: The second half began with Uzbekistan making a clear attacking adjustment, bringing on fresh legs at the break and moving Fayzullaev from the right to the left to attack the space behind Munoz. Colombia remained patient in possession, then spent spells in a low block as Uzbekistan pushed into their half, with the match turning on transition moments and set-piece pressure. Fayzullaev was central to Uzbekistan’s threat, but Colombia’s defensive responses and conservative ball circulation helped them stay in control until the decisive goals arrived.

formations_blocks: Colombia sitting off in a deeper defensive block, allowing Uzbekistan midfield possession
Uzbekistan trying to sustain possession in midfield but struggling to progress
Late-phase stoppage-heavy, low-tempo open play with both sides largely in settled shape
adjustments: Uzbekistan made a double substitution: Ashurmatov and Fayzullaev off, Urozov and Amonov on
Colombia made a double substitution: Puerta and Suarez off, Rios and Hernandez on
Colombia became more passive after the drinks break, hanging back and inviting Uzbekistan on
Colombia slowed the game down at set pieces, taking time over a corner
key_matchups: Diaz vs Khusanov on the left leading to a free-kick
Munoz vs Shomorudov in the duel that ended with an interception and a needless slide
Khamdamov vs Colombia’s defensive set-up on the edge of the box
Mozgovoy vs Colombia’s defensive line/box protection in the 90th-minute chance
phases.summary: This phase was dominated by stoppages, substitutions and a noticeable drop in tempo, with Colombia happy to sit deeper and concede midfield possession. Uzbekistan could not turn that territorial control into meaningful pressure, while Colombia remained the more dangerous side in transition and from set plays, eventually killing the game late with another goal.
